title: qPCR assay for Aquarickettsia spp.
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"4ptc","text":"qPCR for the quantification of Aquarickettsia spp. (Klinges et al., 2022) a putative parasite found in the coral A. cervicornis. This protocol has been altered by incorporating a recently published A. cervicornis CAM control gene (Palacio-Castro et al., 2021) targeted to detect differences across A. cervicornis genotypes because it is a single-copy gene in A. cervicornis.

steps: Remove PCR reagents from freezer and allow reagents to thaw on ice or at room temperature. Wipe down PCR hood with 10% bleach and ethanol. Place consumables such as tubes, plates, plate sealers, and water in PCR hood and turn on UV light for 00:20:00 Once everything is thawed vortex PCR reagents, spin them down, and place them on ice. Keep reagents cool or on ice during the duration of the protocol.
Select SYBER green and long run A B C D 1 Procedure Temperature Time Cycle 2 Initial denaturation 95 C 3 min 1 3 Denaturation 95 C 15 sec 40 4 Annealing 60 C 1 min 40 5 Extension 72 C 30 sec 40

Prepare enough master mix for the number of reactions needed. Each combination of sample and target (gene) should be run at least in duplicates. Add a few reactions to your calculations to account for pipetting errors. A. cervicornis (CAM) master mix A B C 1 Component Volume per Rxn x rxn + 10% 2 PCR water 2.4 uL 3 PrimeTime MM 5 uL 4 Forward primer (10 uM) 0.2 uL 5 Reverse primer (10 uM) 0.2 uL 6 Probe (10 uM) 0.2 uL � 7 Total MM volume per reaction 8 uL � A. rohwerii (tlc1) master mix A B C 1 Component Volume per Rxn x rxn + 10% 2 PCR water 2.4 uL 3 PrimeTime MM 5 uL 4 Forward primer (10 uM) 0.2 uL 5 Reverse primer (10 uM) 0.2 uL 6 Probe (10 uM) 0.2 uL � 7 Total MM volume per reaction 8 uL � Combine all the PCR master-mix reagents in a microcentrifuge tube Mix gently and spin down to collect mixture and remove bubbles

Add 8 uL of master mix to each well. Aiming for the bottom of the well will help to visualize what wells had master mix and DNA added. Add DNA to each well (2 uL). Aiming for the top of the well will help to visualize what wells had master mix and DNA added. Close the plate with optical clear caps or seals Spin down the plate to mix the DNA and mastermix. Place in the qPCR machine and start the machine using the specified settings.
creator: Stephanie Rosales
